decisions.decision: Integrate features into ForceDirectedGraph (the active component) rather than replacing it with BayesNetGraph or building a new component
alternatives_rejected: Replace ForceDirectedGraph with BayesNetGraph — would lose D3 force layout and drag/zoom
Build a third graph component — unnecessary duplication when ForceDirectedGraph just needs feature ports
Merge both graph components — too risky and creates a monolithic component

decisions.decision: Wire ForceDirectedGraph onNodeClick to navigate/select market for existing CptPanel rather than building a new CPT editor
alternatives_rejected: Build inline CPT editing inside graph nodes — too complex and CptPanel already handles this
Open CptPanel as a modal — CptPanel already renders as a panel, modal would break existing UX

decisions.decision: Port useAnimationPropagation integration from BayesNetGraph into ForceDirectedGraph using the same CSS keyframe approach
alternatives_rejected: Rewrite animation with framer-motion — adds unnecessary dependency when CSS keyframes already work
Skip animation in ForceDirectedGraph — core feature requirement left unmet

decisions.decision: Create a GraphToolbar component to house export/import buttons and view switcher, reusing existing networkExport/networkImport utilities
alternatives_rejected: Embed toolbar directly in ForceDirectedGraph — violates separation of concerns and bloats the component
Add buttons to the existing UndoRedoToolbar — mixes unrelated concerns

decisions.decision: Keep undo/redo scoped to local state only, using existing HistoryContext and command pattern
alternatives_rejected: Extend undo/redo to server mutations — backend has no reversal endpoints and mutations may not be idempotent

decisions.decision: Add a view switcher toggle between ForceDirectedGraph and BayesNetGraph, allowing both to coexist as alternative views
alternatives_rejected: Only show ForceDirectedGraph — loses circular layout option that may be useful for small networks
Only show BayesNetGraph — loses D3 force layout with drag/zoom

steps.task: Add selectedMarketId state to MarketDetail and wire ForceDirectedGraph onNodeClick to update it, passing selectedMarketId to CptPanel so clicking a graph node shows that node's CPT

Integrate useAnimationPropagation into ForceDirectedGraph: import the hook, apply CSS animation classes to SVG node/edge elements based on animatingNodes/animatingEdges sets, port CSS keyframes (evidence-pulse, propagation-arrive, edge-flow) and SVG filter definitions from BayesNetGraph

Create GraphToolbar component with export button (calls downloadJson from networkExport), import button (hidden file input + readAndValidateFile from networkImport), and graph view toggle (force-directed vs circular)

Integrate GraphToolbar into MarketDetail page: render above the graph area, connect import success to React Query cache invalidation for graph data refresh, wire view toggle state to conditionally render ForceDirectedGraph or BayesNetGraph

Connect assumption changes to trigger propagation animation in ForceDirectedGraph: listen to AssumptionContext changes and call triggerAnimation with the evidence node ID when assumptions are added/changed

Verify useUndoRedoKeyboard is active within ForceDirectedGraph context, ensure keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Z, Ctrl+Shift+Z) work when graph is focused, add integration tests for the full workflow (click node → edit CPT → undo → export)

decomposition_assessment.decision_reason: Estimated 440 total lines is under the 500-line threshold. Zero high-risk steps (only 1 medium-risk). While steps 1-3 are parallelizable, they all touch MarketDetail.tsx integration points and share graph component context, making parallel child spawning less beneficial than sequential integration. The work is primarily wiring existing components together — most features (CptPanel, HistoryContext, networkExport/Import, useAnimationPropagation) are fully implemented but disconnected. Integration work benefits from a single agent maintaining coherent state across the page-level wiring.
